{"query":"Novak Djokovic official warning Wimbledon July 5 2026 language","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1527,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/novak-djokovic-receives-warning-behavior-145705870.html","title":"Novak Djokovic Receives 'Warning' For Behavior at Wimbledon - Yahoo Sports","content":"Novak Djokovic received an official "warning" for his behavior at Wimbledon on Sunday, July 5.
The official at Centre Court issued a verbal warning to Djokovic, who was officially called out for inappropriate language on the court.
But it's been a frustrating match at times on Sunday.
Djokovic received an official audible obscenity warning after being broken in the third set.
With Carlos Alcaraz not playing due to injury, we could be heading for a Jannik Sinner vs.
"With no Alcaraz, the path would seem clear for Sinner to repeat his triumph of 12 months ago."},{"url":"https://www.express.co.uk/sport/tennis/2225565/Novak-Djokovic-Wimbledon-rant-BBC","title":"Novak Djokovic's sweary Wimbledon rant leaves BBC stunned as umpire steps in | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk","content":"Novak Djokovic got a code violation for audible obscenity (Image: BBC)
7 seed was broken early in the third set and appeared to scream “bulls***” and another word in Serbian, prompting chair umpire Timo Janzen to take action.
John McEnroe, who was commentating on the match live for the BBC, was left confused over what Djokovic had said, believing it was all in his native language.
As the 39-year-old headed to his chair for the changeover, McEnroe noted that Djokovic may have been spurred on by his brief rant.
“Djokovic let it out, got a warning, and just capitalised immediately.
Things went from bad to worse for the 24-time Major champion, who lost the third set 6-3.
You can check out at any time."},{"url":"https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/tennis/novak-djokovic-wimbledon-tirade-bbc-37390372","title":"Novak Djokovic unleashes foul-mouthed Wimbledon tirade as BBC shocked - The Mirror","content":"Novak Djokovic was handed a code violation warning during the third set of his Wimbledon clash with Roman Safiullin.
The Serbian icon fought back from 2-5 down in the first set to claim it via a tiebreak, before appearing on course to cruise towards victory after establishing a two-set advantage over the Russian qualifier.
This prompted chair umpire Timo Janzen to intervene and remind the former champ of his responsibilities.
When you hear another language, it immediately goes to your native tongue.
"There was no complaint from Djokovic, so," Tim Henman added during the BBC's live coverage.
The brief moment of frustration appeared to galvanise the seven-time former champion.
The world No.132 required an on-court medical timeout while leading 3-2 but eventually found another gear to win the set, only for Djokovic to claim the fourth set and with it the match.
Although Djokovic appeared to have rediscovered his form, Henman and McEnroe were unconvinced he was content.

Wimbledon 2026 is just around the corner and Seat Unique has tickets on sale now with VIP access."}]}
